description: A source watchlist plus recent research signals for AI infrastructure: serving systems, agent benchmarks, RAG evaluation, tool protocols, observability, security, and programmable LM systems.

recent_signals.title: Riot announces first data center lease with AMD at Rockdale
recent_signals.signal: Riot is converting bitcoin-mining power capacity into AI/HPC leasing, with AMD as an anchor tenant and expansion potential to 200 MW critical IT load.
recent_signals.map_implication: The scarce input is buyer-trustable, GPU-ready, power-secured capacity, not only raw megawatts.

recent_signals.title: IREN signs $9.7 billion agreement with Microsoft to deploy AI cloud infrastructure
recent_signals.signal: Bitcoin-miner-style power assets can convert into hyperscaler AI cloud capacity when they can supply liquid-cooled GPU infrastructure at scale.
recent_signals.map_implication: Miner-to-AI conversion should be represented as a supply-side subcategory inside Datacenter, Power & Cooling.

recent_signals.title: GridCARE speed to power for AI
recent_signals.signal: Power acceleration for AI data centers is becoming software-enabled utility infrastructure rather than generic site brokerage.
recent_signals.map_implication: The power-capacity opportunity is partial: utility-side latent-capacity discovery has a visible player, while site diligence and miner conversion remain less standardized.

recent_signals.title: Microsoft Agent 365
recent_signals.signal: Microsoft is packaging agent observability, governance, and security into a control plane tied to Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Security.
recent_signals.map_implication: Agent governance is being absorbed by platform IAM/security incumbents, which lowers the standalone startup wedge.

recent_signals.title: Okta for AI Agents
recent_signals.signal: Agent identity is becoming an enterprise IAM product category: registration, ownership, credential rotation, access policies, and retirement are now first-class controls.
recent_signals.map_implication: The agent-permission layer should be marked contested, not open; the remaining white space is cross-vendor action receipts and delegated-intent policy.

recent_signals.title: From GPT-4 to AGI: Counting the OOMs
recent_signals.signal: Progress is framed as the compound effect of raw compute, algorithmic efficiency, and unhobbling from chatbot to agent.
recent_signals.map_implication: The atlas needs separate surfaces for physical compute, model supply, orchestration/runtime, and vertical applications.

recent_signals.title: Racing to the Trillion-Dollar Cluster
recent_signals.signal: AI buildout is constrained by energized datacenter capacity, power, advanced packaging, HBM, fabs, and other physical supply chains.
recent_signals.map_implication: Semiconductor supply, datacenter power, and industrial materials deserve first-class cells, not just tags under compute.

recent_signals.title: Lock Down the Labs: Security for AGI
recent_signals.signal: Model weights, algorithmic secrets, hardware supply chains, and datacenter operations become national-security assets as capabilities rise.
recent_signals.map_implication: Security/governance should span software policy, weights, people, hardware provenance, and physical datacenter protection.

recent_signals.title: The Project
recent_signals.signal: The frontier AI stack may become a government-scale mobilization involving labs, clouds, chips, power, and secure facilities.
recent_signals.map_implication: Defense/security is a vertical-facing slice across compute, cloud, model labs, governance, and industrial inputs.

recent_signals.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
recent_signals.authors: Woosuk Kwon, Zhuohan Li, Siyuan Zhuang, Ying Sheng, Lianmin Zheng, Cody Hao Yu, Joseph E. Gonzalez, Hao Zhang & Ion Stoica
recent_signals.signal: KV-cache management became a first-class serving bottleneck, making inference infra look more like an operating-systems market.
recent_signals.map_implication: Serving and routing companies should be judged on memory, batching, latency, and utilization, not just model coverage.

recent_signals.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
recent_signals.signal: Agents need multi-turn interactive benchmarks, not only static language tasks.
recent_signals.map_implication: Agent runtime winners will need eval loops and environment-specific traces baked in.

recent_signals.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
recent_signals.authors: Carlos E. Jimenez, John Yang, Alexander Wettig, Shunyu Yao, Kexin Pei, Ofir Press & Karthik Narasimhan
recent_signals.signal: Software engineering became a durable, executable benchmark for long-context, multi-file agent work.
recent_signals.map_implication: Coding has the clearest eval-to-product bridge; other vertical agents still need similar task suites.

recent_signals.title: RAGAS: Automated Evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation
recent_signals.authors: Shahul Es, Jithin James, Luis Espinosa-Anke & Steven Schockaert
recent_signals.signal: RAG quality decomposes into retrieval relevance, faithfulness, and answer quality.
recent_signals.map_implication: Retrieval, eval, and observability are converging into one product surface for enterprise RAG.

recent_signals.title: DSPy: Compiling Declarative Language Model Calls into Self-Improving Pipelines
recent_signals.signal: Prompting is being recast as a programmable, optimizable system.
recent_signals.map_implication: The orchestration layer may look less like prompt templates and more like compilers, optimizers, and typed programs.

recent_signals.title: Introducing the Model Context Protocol
recent_signals.signal: Tool and data integrations are becoming a protocol layer.
recent_signals.map_implication: Agent infrastructure will need registries, auth, permissions, and security scanning around tool protocols.

recent_signals.title: A Taxonomy of AgentOps for Enabling Observability of Foundation Model based Agents
recent_signals.signal: AgentOps needs traceable artifacts across the agent production lifecycle.
recent_signals.map_implication: Observability for agents is not just logs: it includes tool calls, plans, memory, evaluations, policies, and human review.

recent_signals.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP): Landscape, Security Threats, and Future Research Directions
recent_signals.signal: Protocol standardization creates a new attack surface around tools, servers, clients, and permission boundaries.
recent_signals.map_implication: The AI firewall category will move from prompt filters toward tool authorization, runtime policy, and supply-chain security.
